llm_route_weight_response.global_rationale: Weights assigned by prioritizing route frequency (days observed) and distance statistics. Higher-frequency clusters receive proportionally larger weights. Distance profiles are validated for consistency: short-haul trucks (155, 1687, 1688, 1506) emphasize clusters with 30–70 km means; moderate-distance trucks balance 80–200 km clusters; long-haul trucks (1312, 1578, 1421) emphasize 250+ km clusters. Rare clusters (1–3 days) receive minimal weights. Variance within clusters is acknowledged: high-variance clusters get reduced weight relative to their frequency if outliers suggest data quality issues. Target vehicle 155 reflects balanced portfolio of short routes (cluster 2, 3) with occasional longer trips (cluster 1).
risks: Cluster 3 in vehicle 1688 (293.6 km mean, only 4 days) appears anomalous relative to dominant 50–70 km profile; weight set to 0.08 to suppress but not eliminate.
Vehicle 1785 cluster 0 shows high variance (24–321 km) suggesting mixed stop patterns; 0.42 weight allows flexibility but may oversample extreme days.
Distance file may contain outliers or data errors (e.g., 1–6 km distances in some clusters); LLM ablation prevents contextual validation.
Appearance frequency-weighted joint probabilities for other vehicles will scale scenario sampling; verify final scenario counts are reasonable.
No zone or time-of-day data available; pure distance-based clustering may conflate unrelated route types.

final_scenario.sampling_method: LLM-adjusted route-cluster weights. Target routes sample from llm_route_cluster_weight; other routes sample from truck_appearance_frequency * llm_route_cluster_weight.
